title: EQ Prep on the Incoming Track
description: Cut the bass on the incoming track before you bring it in — prevents bass clash and keeps the mix clean.

requiredTracks.characteristics: both tracks should have prominent bass elements

steps.instruction: Watch: Deck A plays at full EQ — all bands at center (0 dB). You hear the full mix.
Watch: before Deck B is introduced, its bass is cut to -24 dB. Now when it fades in, there is no bass clash.
Watch: Deck B fades in at 80% volume — only mids and highs from Deck B blend with Deck A's full spectrum.
Watch: now Deck B's bass is restored at the phrase boundary. Deck A's bass is killed simultaneously — a clean swap.

phases.instruction: Two bass guitars in the same room create a muddy war. Before the second player enters, they mute their bass. When the moment is right, Player A mutes — Player B unmutes. Clean handoff, one clear bass sound at all times.

steps.instruction: Deck A is playing. Before you do anything else, confirm Deck B's LOW EQ is cut to -24 dB.
Start playing Deck B and raise its volume to 80%. Listen — no bass clash because Deck B's lows are cut.
Listen to the blend. Deck A provides bass and full energy. Deck B adds mids and highs on top.
Wait for a phrase boundary. When the phrase resets — kill Deck A's bass and restore Deck B's bass simultaneously.
Restore Deck B's bass to center (0 dB). Deck B now drives the low end alone.
Fade Deck A's volume to zero. The transition is complete — no mud, no clash, clean handoff.
